text:
Brenda Villa's story is fascinating.

From growing up in the City of Commerce to playing boys water polo at Bell Gardens High, Villa says, "... water polo found me."

She was aÂ 4-time 1st team All-League selection, 4-time 1st team All-CIF selection, and 4-time All-American at Bell Gardens ... and that was just the beginning.

Villa ended up playing at Stanford where she won theÂ Peter J. Cutino AwardÂ in 2002, which is like the Heisman Trophy in college water polo. She then went on to compete for team USA in four Olympics - 2000 Sydney, 2004 Athens, 2008 Beijing and 2012 London.

"It was everything you could imagine and more," Villa said of her Olympic experience. "I was at the first (Olympics) ever for women's water polo."

Villa won silver, bronze, silver and gold in the four Olympics, respectively.Â

Villa has won four Olympic medals, four World Championship medals, three Pan American Games medals, eight FINA World League medals and one FINA World Cup.

Of those 20 competitions, Villa won gold in 15 of them, making herÂ the most decorated athlete in the world of womenâs water polo.

Villa was named Female Water Polo Player of the Decade for 2000-2009 by the FINA Aquatics World Magazine. She is a leading goalscorer in Olympic water polo history, with 31 goals, and inÂ 2018, was inducted into the International Swimming Hall of Fame and the USA Water Polo Hall of Fame.

Despite winning gold in London, Villa says the Sydney games were her favorite.

"Being part of history and competing in the first ever. No one else besides the athletes there can say that," Villa said.

Villa is now the varsity girls water polo coach at Orange Lutheran. The Lancers won the CIF Southern Section Open Division championship in 2023, Villa's first year at the helm.Â

Orange Lutheran is on the quest for a second straight.

Tarek Fattal has been covering high school sports since 2015 in Southern California and primarily in Los Angeles, coveringÂ notable athletes such as Bronny James, Kayvon Thibodeaux and Alyssa Thompson.Â He was with the LA Daily News for eight years, which included being the beat reporter for the UCLA men's basketball team. Tarek can be seen on TV regularly on CBS/KCAL as a sports analyst with Jim Hill.
